videos.title: New to Major League Pickleball? Watch this first.
videos.description:
It’s not just pickleball, it’s team pickleball.
Coed lineups. City pride. Matches that come down to a final DreamBreaker™.

This is Major League Pickleball, where team chemistry is the difference maker and every event builds toward the hardest trophy to win in the sport. 

20 teams. The 2026 season takes place over 13 events this summer. Join us at an event or tune-in and catch your favorite team.

videos.title: 2026 MLP Draft presented by Skechers
videos.description:

Expect real-time reactions, insights, and a front row seat to understanding teams' selections.
⁠

videos.title: What is Pickleball?
videos.description:
Pickleball is one of the fastest growing sports in the United States and for good reason. In this video, we break down what Pickleball is, why it’s so popular, and how you can get started playing.

Pickleball is a paddle sport that combines elements of tennis, table tennis, and badminton into one fun and accessible game. One of its biggest appeals is that players of all ages and skill levels can p

videos.title: Pickleball Fundamentals For Beginners
videos.description:
Having a strong foundation in pickleball fundamentals will set you up for long-term success on the court. In this video, we break down some of the most important fundamentals every beginner should learn to start playing with confidence.

First, it all starts with your grip. We recommend the continental grip, which you can find by forming a “V” with your dominant hand and placing it along the edge

videos.title: Most Important Rules to Start Playing Pickleball
videos.description:
If you don’t know the rules, you can’t play! In this video, we break down the most important pickleball rules so you can step on the court with confidence and start playing right away.

First, understand the boundaries of the court. The baselines and sidelines mark the limits, and for a ball to be in, it must land on or within these lines. If any part of the ball touches the line, it’s considered

videos.title: Volley Fundamentals in Pickleball
videos.description:
The volley is one of the most important and versatile shots in pickleball. In this video, we break down volley fundamentals so you can improve your control, reaction time, and ability to win points at the net.

A volley is any shot hit out of the air before the ball bounces. These shots can be offensive or defensive, hard or soft, but if you’re making contact before it hits the ground, it’s a voll

This highlight reel showcases the intense rallies, standout sportsmanship and the vibrant USA Pickleball community atmosphere that make

items.title: The Pickleball Shot You're Not Using Enough: The Poach
items.summary: The pickleball poach isn't random aggression—it's a calculated read of your opponent's patterns and positioning. Master this net-dominating shot to apply pressure and force weak returns.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: Simple, Proven Dink Patterns to Win More Pickleball Points
items.summary: Most pickleball players default to the same cross-court dink pattern over and over, which actually hurts their game. Master these three dink patterns instead to force more errors and win more points.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: Pickleball Etiquette for Beginners: The Unwritten Rules That Matter
items.summary: Pickleball etiquette for beginners goes beyond the rulebook, the unwritten social code of the court matters just as much as your backhand. Here's what every new player needs to know before stepping on a court.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: Pickleball Strategy Evolution: What's Changed in 9 Years of Tournament Play
items.summary: Jordan Briones breaks down his gold medal match from 9 years ago, revealing how pickleball strategy has fundamentally shifted. From dinking dominance to aggressive drives, the evolution of pickleball strategy shows why the game looks completely different today.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: The Complete Guide for a Better Backhand in Pickleball
items.summary: Your backhand technique doesn't have to be a liability. Universal Rackets breaks down the exact mechanics, drills, and mindset shifts that transform your backhand from your weakest shot into your most reliable weapon on court.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: Master the Pickleball Flick: Complete Technique Guide
items.summary: The pickleball flick is one of the most effective attacking shots in the game, allowing you to generate topspin and catch opponents off guard. Master both backhand and forehand variations with this comprehensive technique breakdown.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: How to Hit the Middle Court Drop Shot in Pickleball
items.summary: Most pickleball players make the same mistake when they find themselves in the middle of the court with an easy ball. Instead of hitting an aggressive shot out wide, the middle court drop shot is the smarter play that gains ground and wins points.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: The Top Seeds Dominate the PPA Tour Finals - Tournament Recap
items.summary: The PPA Finals took place this past weekend at the Life Time Rancho San Clemente in California to cap off the 2025-2026 PPA Tour season.
items.author: Erik Tice

items.title: The Best Dressed Pros at the Toys “R” Us PPA Finals
items.summary: Between the high-level matches and standout spring style, the PPA season's final event did not disappoint.
items.author: Emily Visnic

items.title: Mizuno's Adjustable Weight Paddle: Game-Changer or Gimmick?
items.summary: Mizuno dropped its first-ever pickleball paddle, and it comes with an innovative feature: an adjustable weight system built right into the frame.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: The 5-Second Reset Every Frustrated Pickleball Player Needs
items.summary: Getting angry at yourself in pickleball is common, but constant yelling or harsh self-talk can make you tighter, distract your partner, and lead to more mistakes. Use a quick reset: name the error, choose one correction, breathe, reconnect with your partner, and move on to the ne
items.author: Ana

items.title: 7 Reliable Ways to Know When to Let the Ball Go Out in Pickleball
items.summary: Let the ball go out in pickleball when it is shoulder-high or rising, hit from below net height, struck with a big backswing, or coming from an off-balance opponent. Also watch court position, spin, angle, and wind. The goal is to stop saving your opponent’s bad attacks. One of t
items.author: Ana

items.title: 2026 PPA Tour Finals recap: Ben Johns, Anna Leigh Waters complete near perfect spring seasons
items.summary: Anna Leigh Waters and Ben Johns put the exclamation point on an incredible run this spring with clean sweeps in the doubles divisions on Sunday at the PPA Tour Finals. More
items.author: The Kitchen

items.title: Pickleball Strategy: 3 Pro Tips to Outsmart Your Opponent
items.summary: Advanced pickleball comes down to reading your opponents and setting traps to lure them into mistakes.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: How to Improve Pickleball Footwork: 5 Drills to Move Like a Pro
items.summary: Pickleball footwork drills are the fastest way to close the gap between your shot-making ability and your actual on-court results. Here are five drills that competitive players use to move faster, recover quicker, and stop giving away free points.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: The Pickleball Performance Hack Most Players Ignore
items.summary: VKTRY Insoles feature a full-length carbon fiber plate you can customize based on your weight and activity type, returning energy with every step. And they'll last far longer than your pricier paddle.
items.author: Alex E. Weaver

items.title: When to Cross the Center Line and Cover Your Partner in Pickleball
items.summary: Most 3.5 to 4.0 pickleball players treat the center line pickleball like an invisible wall they can't cross. This fundamental misunderstanding is costing them matches and limiting their doubles strategy. Here's what you need to know to fix it.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: How to Find Pickleball Courts Near You: Every Free Tool and Resource
items.summary: If you're trying to find pickleball courts near me, you have more options than ever, from the official USA Pickleball court finder to apps like Pickleheads, Places2Play, and DUPR. This guide covers every free tool and local hack to get you on the court fast.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: How Long After Surgery Before You Can Play Pickleball Again?
items.summary: Most rec players can return to pickleball after surgery, but timelines vary. Knee or hip replacements often take 3–6 months for controlled doubles, while meniscus repairs, rotator cuff repairs, and ACL surgery may take longer. Return should depend on pain, swelling, strength, bal
items.author: Ana

items.title: How to Counter Like a 5.0 in Pickleball
items.summary: To counter in pickleball like a higher-level player, keep your paddle out front, use a stable wrist, stay balanced, and cover likely attack zones. Counter balls into your prepared paddle or chest-height zone, but reset or let go balls that are low, late, outside your frame, or sa
items.author: Ana

items.title: The $225 Million Pickleball Deal Nobody Explained to the 24 Million People Who Actually Play
items.summary: You probably saw the headline last week. Pickleball Inc. raised $225 million. “A seismic day,” said Connor Pardoe, the PPA Tour founder who now runs the whole operation. CNBC covered it. CBS covered it. Yahoo Sports covered it. What none of them covered is what the deal actually
items.author: Paul Lemley

items.title: Tama Shimabukuro: Pickleball's newest superstar discusses Atlanta run that changed everything
items.summary: We wanted to learn more about Tama Shimabukuro, so earlier this week we invited him on PBallers, our remote version of Pickleballers. More
items.author: The Kitchen

items.title: 2026 PPA Tour Finals: Format, pools and schedule
items.summary: This week's PPA Tour Finals serve as the season-ending championship for the top players on tour. More
items.author: The Kitchen

items.title: How to Attack the Triangle Pattern in Pickleball Doubles
items.summary: The pickleball triangle pattern is a doubles strategy where you attack one player’s jam zone, then anticipate the likely counter lane. Instead of speeding up randomly, you target the inside hip, shoulder, chicken wing, or lower body to create a predictable next ball for you or yo
items.author: Ana

items.title: How to Turn Your Punch Volley Into a Finish Shot
items.summary: To turn a pickleball punch volley into a winner, take the ball early, keep your wrist stable, stay compact, and move the paddle forward through contact. Aim at the opponent’s feet, hip, middle seam, or open space instead of swinging harder. The goal is controlled pressure, not a
items.author: Ana

items.title: Can Watching Pro Pickleball Actually Make You Better?
items.summary: Watching pro pickleball can improve your game if you watch with a purpose. Instead of copying highlight shots, study footwork, positioning, shot selection, recovery, patience, and how pros build points. Pick one player, one skill, and one habit to apply in your next rec game. Yes
items.author: Ana

items.title: I watched every episode of the new pro pickleball docuseries 'Partners' -- here's what I learned
items.summary: A new reality series about pro pickleball called "Partners" was released Tuesday and is now available on the PPA Tour’s YouTube Channel, Amazon Prime and Pickleball TV. More
items.author: The Kitchen

items.title: This epic house with pickleball court in Lakeland, Florida, could be yours for $2.69M
items.summary: A house located in Lakeland, Florida, that features an epic pickleball court hit the market recently for $2.69 million. Check out photos and view the listing. More
items.author: The Kitchen

items.title: How to help families of plane crash victims who were heading to pickleball tournament
items.summary: Fundraisers and meal trains have been set up for the families of the five people who were killed in a Texas plane crash on their way to a pickleball tournament last week. More
items.author: The Kitchen

items.title: What to Do When Nobody Dinks in Rec Pickleball
items.summary: When nobody dinks in rec pickleball, don’t force soft rallies. Use deep returns, blocks, resets, low middle balls, crosscourt dinks, and smart counters to make hard attacks less comfortable. With non-dinking partners, keep cues simple and focus on one controllable soft ball at a
items.author: Ana

items.title: How to Read Where Your Opponent Will Hit the Ball in Pickleball
items.summary: To read where your opponent will hit in pickleball, don’t just watch the paddle face. Read the contact window, shoulders, feet, balance, court geometry, and likely counter lanes. These cues help you narrow the possible shots earlier, react faster, and stop guessing during rallies
items.author: Ana

items.title: 2026 Atlanta Pickleball Championships: Tama Shimabukuro steals the spotlight, but top seeds roll in finals
items.summary: The favorites may have prevailed in every division on Championship Sunday at the PPA Tour Atlanta Pickleball Championships, but in a few years pickleball fans could look back on this week as the tournament Tama Shimabukuro became a star. More
items.author: The Kitchen

items.title: Apollo Sports Capital leads $225M investment in parent company of PPA Tour, Major League Pickleball
items.summary: Pickleball Inc. has become the new parent company of the PPA Tour and Major League Pickleball following a $225 million investment led by Apollo Global Management’s sports fund, Apollo Sports Capital. More
items.author: The Kitchen

items.title: How PodPlay is helping pickleball facilities grow their business
items.summary: PodPlay is a comprehensive management system that allows facility operators to handle court bookings, payments, leagues and events all in one place. More
items.author: The Kitchen

items.title: Ranking the Top 10 pickleball events to attend
items.summary: Whether it's the amateur experience or the chance to watch the best pro players compete on scenic Championship Courts, these are the Top 10 pickleball events to attend. More
items.author: The Kitchen

items.title: PPA Tour 2026 Atlanta Pickleball Championships: Draws, schedule and storylines
items.summary: The Atlanta Pickleball Championships is annually one of the biggest events on the PPA Tour calendar, and the action started Monday with pro qualifying. More
items.author: The Kitchen

items.title: UPA-A releases new rulebook that will govern PPA Tour, Major League Pickleball matches
items.summary: The United Pickleball Association of America recently released its first official rulebook that specifically applies to the PPA Tour and Major League Pickleball. More
items.author: The Kitchen

items.title: Q&A with rising young pickleball pro Cailyn Campbell
items.summary: Cailyn Campbell has been on the pro pickleball scene for more than a year, but she's still only 15 years old and is rapidly climbing the PPA Tour rankings. More
items.author: The Kitchen

items.title: Proton paddles OK'd for use in PPA Tour, Major League Pickleball events, officials say
items.summary: Proton Pickleball and the United Pickleball Association have resolved their contract dispute, and Proton paddles are now permitted for use in PPA Tour and Major League Pickleball events going forward. More
items.author: The Kitchen

items.title: Anna Bright reveals which pro players have impressed her the most in 2026
items.summary: In a recent newsletter, Anna Bright went into detail on which pro players have impressed her the most in 2026. More
